preferredDeck: Reduces Settings work, sheds feed media earlier under WebKit pressure, and keeps today's Desktop performance fixes intact.
editorialGuidance: Keep the tone concise, professional, and specific.
Lead with user-facing outcomes, shipping milestones, trust wins, and installability improvements.
Demote internal cleanup unless it clearly changes the shipped product.
pinnedHighlights: Settings now lazy-mounts section bodies, suppresses expensive blur while actively scrolling or searching, and has a dedicated 1,600-source performance guard.
Feed cards now shed inline media when aggregate WebKit RSS reaches 1.5 GB, which matches the pressure range seen in the live dev soak before the old 2 GB threshold fired.
Friends graph Fit all now waits for dense layout completion before applying.
Dense Map fallback rendering reduces visible marker paint work during wheel and pointer movement.
Shows the fatal recovery screen instead of a blank shell when a React render error is caught.
Restores WebKit WebContent memory attribution for current launch renderer processes.
Relaxes hidden renderer watchdog recovery so hidden or backgrounded windows are not recycled too aggressively.
Improves dense Friends graph recovery, Map interaction smoothness, desktop crash recovery, and WebKit memory attribution for today's dev builds
Show crash recovery when rendering fails
Keeps hidden sessions quieter and restores WebKit memory attribution
editorialNotes: Latest dev build adds Settings scale and feed media pressure fixes on top of the earlier same-day performance work.
